finding.summary: The track exhibits a dark tonal balance with a strong low-frequency emphasis: 100 Hz is 6 dB louder than 1 kHz and 12 dB louder than 4 kHz.
finding.probable_cause: The captured distorted guitar has excessive low-end energy, likely from the instrument tone or the Drive plugin, leading to a muffled sound.
finding.confidence_reason: The isolated capture has very little silence (0.05 fraction), and the 1/3-octave spectrum provides clear, reliable evidence of the imbalance.

evidence_refs.description: 100 Hz band level is -18 dBFS, much higher than mid/high bands.
1 kHz band level is -24 dBFS, indicating a roll-off above the bass region.
4 kHz band level is -30 dBFS, confirming the dark tilt.

proposal.reason: The FX chain contains only a drive plugin with no EQ; adjusting the Drive parameter cannot reliably rebalance the frequency spectrum. Without an equalizer, there is no actionable control to address the tonal imbalance.
